title: lagunaria cuneiformis
intro: Lagunaria cuneiformis, also known as the Coral Tree or Buttonwood, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malvaceae, characterized by its distinctive heart-shaped leaves and vibrant pink or white flowers. Traditionally, Lagunaria cuneiformis has been used in herbal medicine to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its rich concentration of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which provide numerous health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immune-modulating effects. In herbal preparations, Lagunaria cuneiformis is often used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and its extracts have been incorporated into various remedies for their therapeutic potential.
